prestazioni: Supercharger upgrade to a Harrop HTV2300 TVS supercharger (replacing the stock Eaton M90) on the AJ26 3.2L supercharged inline-6 (engine code: AJ26), paired with a custom intercooler from Bell Auto Services and a 3.5" pulley for 12-14 psi boost, pushing power to ~520 HP and 480 lb-ft torque with supporting fuel upgrades (Walbro 450 LPH pump, ID1000x injectors, and a Motec M150 ECU)

Full titanium exhaust system by Milltek Sport with 3" mandrel-bent downpipes, 200-cell catalytic converters, and a valved rear section to retain OEM refinement while reducing backpressure by 40% for improved throttle response and a deeper, more aggressive tone

Suspension overhaul with Öhlins Road & Track DFV coilovers (custom valved for the DB7’s unique double-wishbone front and multi-link rear geometry), paired with polybushings from SuperPro (part #SPF1001K) and a Whiteline front sway bar (24mm adjustable) to eliminate body roll and sharpen turn-in for Nürburgring-level handling
estetica: Full carbon fiber widebody conversion using the Liberty Walk LB-DB7 kit (hand-laid pre-preg carbon, 3M matte finish), extending fenders by 2.5" front and 3" rear to accommodate 19x10" ET25 (front) and 19x12" ET35 (rear) BBS LM wheels with Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires (265/35/19 front, 305/30/19 rear)

Custom front splitter and rear diffuser by Anderson Composites (Aston Martin DB7-specific molds), paired with a GT3-style rear wing from Vorsteiner (carbon fiber, 3-position adjustable) to generate 120 kg of downforce at 120 mph while maintaining the DB7’s iconic silhouette

Full interior rewrap in Bridge of Weir leather (color-matched to Aston’s ‘Cinnabar Orange’ with contrast stitching) by Royal Steering Wheels, featuring a MOMO Prototipo Evo 350mm suede wheel, custom carbon fiber gauge pods (housing AIM MXS Strada digital dash), and Recaro Pole Position seats (recliners removed, fixed-back for weight savings)
